name: Luxembourg
avoid: Grey late-autumn and deep-winter weeks if you want long hiking days in Mullerthal — trails feel damp, daylight is short and smaller towns wind down early

cities.name: Luxembourg City
cities.notes: UNESCO old quarters, deep valleys, museums and trams with easy bases for day trips across the country

cities.name: Vianden
cities.notes: Storybook castle town on the Our with chairlift views, Victor Hugo links and a compact medieval centre

cities.name: Echternach
cities.notes: Luxembourg's oldest town with abbey heritage, relaxed squares and a practical base for the eastern hiking belt

cities.name: Mullerthal
cities.notes: Little Switzerland landscapes with forested trails, rock gorges and the photogenic Schiessentümpel bridge

itinerary.sim: Orange Holiday Europe eSIM or Airalo Eurolink both work well in Luxembourg and neighboring EU countries — expect roughly ₹1,800–₹2,700 for 10–12GB.

Luxembourg City.name: Luxembourg City
Luxembourg City.note: Two days is ideal for the UNESCO centre plus Kirchberg museums; a third day works if you want more galleries or a slower café-and-viewpoint pace.

activities.name: Bock Casemates
activities.tip: Adult ticket is €11; book a timed slot in summer because the tunnels cap visitor numbers.

activities.name: Chemin de la Corniche and old fortification viewpoints
Grand Ducal Palace exterior, Place Guillaume II and Notre-Dame Cathedral walk
Grund riverside stroll and Neumünster Abbey courtyard
MUDAM Luxembourg
activities.tip: Adult ticket is €10; the museum pairs well with the nearby Fort Thüngen and Philharmonie area.

activities.name: Dräi Eechelen Museum and Fort Thüngen exterior grounds
Pfaffenthal Panoramic Elevator and lower-town walk
Luxembourg City History Museum

Vianden.name: Vianden
Vianden.note: Vianden is perfect as an overnight or long day trip; stay longer only if you want slow riverside time, extra hiking or the chairlift twice.

days.theme: Castle Hill, Victor Hugo & River Views

activities.name: Vianden Castle
activities.tip: Adult ticket is €13; arrive early for quieter ramparts and valley panoramas.

activities.name: Victor Hugo House Literary Museum
Vianden chairlift for ridge views over the Our valley
Ourdall promenade and upper-town lanes

Echternach.name: Echternach
Echternach.note: Echternach works well as a calm base for the east: abbey heritage by day, lakeside walks in the evening and short bus hops into the trail zone.

activities.name: Basilica of St Willibrord and Abbey courtyard
Echternach Abbey Museum
Market square, old lanes and town-wall fragments
Echternach Lake promenade or pedal-boat break

Mullerthal.name: Mullerthal
Mullerthal.note: Give Mullerthal a full day for the classic short hikes; add another if you want longer trail sections, cave-like rock passages or a slower rural stay.

activities.name: Schiessentümpel waterfall and stone bridge
Mullerthal Trail loop through Wollefsschlucht and forested rock passages
Heringer Millen visitor stop and local produce break
Aquatower Berdorf

connections.method: CFL train from Luxembourg to Ettelbruck, then RGTR/CFL bus onward to Vianden (about 70–90 min total)
Bus via Diekirch or Ettelbruck into Echternach (about 75–95 min total depending on connection)
RGTR bus from Echternach to Mullerthal or Berdorf trailheads (about 20–30 min)
RGTR bus back to Luxembourg City via Echternach or Junglinster (about 55–75 min)

extras: Luxembourg's biggest budget advantage is free nationwide public transport in second class: CFL trains, RGTR buses, trams and city buses are all free, so intercity hops usually cost ₹0.
Bock Casemates, MUDAM and Vianden Castle are the three headline paid sights here; otherwise many of the best experiences are scenic walks, viewpoints and small-town wandering.
Mullerthal trails get slippery after rain, so pack shoes with grip and avoid rushing the rock passages late in the day.
Luxembourg City has the broadest restaurant choice by far; Vianden, Echternach and Mullerthal feel much quieter after dinner, especially outside peak summer weekends.
Check Monday and Tuesday openings before you plan museum-heavy days because smaller heritage sites sometimes shorten hours early in the week.

Luxembourg City: Chemin de la Corniche Luxembourg City
